title: Mapps init Stalling

OS: macOS (Apple Silicon/darwin-arm64)
Node Versions Used: Tried Node v22.14.0 and Node v20.19.0 (using nvm).
CLI Version: @mondaycom/apps-cl…

posts.author: mikefails (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml:
• Goal: Trying to run mapps init to set up a new local project for monday Code.
  • OS: macOS (Apple Silicon/darwin-arm64)
  • Node Versions Used: Tried Node v22.14.0 and Node v20.19.0 (using nvm).
  • CLI Version: @mondaycom/apps-cli/4.6.0
  • Problem:** When running mapps init inside the project directory, it prompts for the API token, successfully creates/updates ~/.config/mapps/.mappsrc, but then immediately exits. It never prompts to select an app to link or a template, and does not create the monday-code.json file.
  • Troubleshooting Done: I’ve confirmed the directory is clean (ls -a), tried reinstalling the CLI (sudo npm uninstall/install), tried both Node v22 and v20, tried the --appId flag, and checked --verbose output (which showed no extra errors after authentication).

Any ideas?
